title: Locate a regression with git bisect
description: Use binary search over history with a reproducible test to pinpoint the exact commit that introduced a defect.
body: Use when a bug appeared somewhere in a range of commits and manual inspection is too slow. Do not guess the culprit when a deterministic check exists. Steps: confirm a good and a bad commit; run git bisect start, mark them; at each step build and run the failing check, marking good or bad; record the commit bisect reports. Validation signals include bisect converging on one commit whose revert removes the symptom. Risk boundary: an unreliable test misleads bisect, so verify the check is deterministic before trusting the result.
